verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc cum dixisset Jesus, egressus est cum discipulis suis trans torrentem Cedron, ubi erat hortus, in quem introivit ipse, et discipuli ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα εἰπὼν ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐξῆλθεν σὺν τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ πέραν τοῦ χειμάῤῥου τῶν κεδρὼν ὅπου ἦν κῆπος εἰς ὃν εἰσῆλθεν αὐτὸς καὶ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: When Jesus had spoken these words, he went forth with his disciples over the brook Cedron, where was a garden, into the which he entered, and his disciples.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sciebat autem et Judas, qui tradebat eum, locum: quia frequenter Jesus convenerat illuc cum discipulis suis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ᾔδει δὲ καὶ ἰούδας ὁ παραδιδοὺς αὐτὸν τὸν τόπον ὅτι πολλάκις συνήχθη ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐκεῖ μετὰ τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And Judas also, which betrayed him, knew the place: for Jesus ofttimes resorted thither with his disciples.

verses.latin_vulgate: Judas ergo cum accepisset cohortem, et a pontificibus et pharisæis ministros, venit illuc cum laternis, et facibus, et armis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ οὖν ἰούδας λαβὼν τὴν σπεῖραν καὶ ἐκ τῶν ἀρχιερέων καὶ φαρισαίων ὑπηρέτας ἔρχεται ἐκεῖ μετὰ φανῶν καὶ λαμπάδων καὶ ὅπλων
verses.king_james: Judas then, having received a band *of men* and officers from the chief priests and Pharisees, cometh thither with lanterns and torches and weapons.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jesus itaque sciens omnia quæ ventura erant super eum, processit, et dixit eis: Quem quæritis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰησοῦς οὖν εἰδὼς πάντα τὰ ἐρχόμενα ἐπ᾽ αὐτὸν ἐξελθὼν εἶπεν αὐτοῖς τίνα ζητεῖτε
verses.king_james: Jesus therefore, knowing all things that should come upon him, went forth, and said unto them, Whom seek ye?

verses.latin_vulgate: Responderunt ei: Jesum Nazarenum. Dicit eis Jesus: Ego sum. Stabat autem et Judas, qui tradebat eum, cum ipsis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθησαν αὐτῷ ἰησοῦν τὸν ναζωραῖον λέγει αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς, ἐγώ εἰμι εἱστήκει δὲ καὶ ἰούδας ὁ παραδιδοὺς αὐτὸν μετ᾽ αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: They answered him, Jesus of Nazareth. Jesus saith unto them, I am *he*. And Judas also, which betrayed him, stood with them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ut ergo dixit eis: Ego sum: abierunt retrorsum, et ceciderunt in terram.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς οὖν εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὅτι ἐγώ εἰμι ἀπῆλθον εἰς τὰ ὀπίσω καὶ ἔπεσον χαμαί
verses.king_james: As soon then as he had said unto them, I am *he*, they went backward, and fell to the ground.

verses.latin_vulgate: Iterum ergo interrogavit eos: Quem quæritis? Illi autem dixerunt: Jesum Nazarenum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάλιν οὖν αὐτούς ἐπηρώτησεν τίνα ζητεῖτε οἱ δὲ εἶπον, ἰησοῦν τὸν ναζωραῖον
verses.king_james: Then asked he them again, Whom seek ye? And they said, Jesus of Nazareth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Jesus: Dixi vobis, quia ego sum: si ergo me quæritis, sinite hos abire.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ὁ ἰησοῦς εἶπον ὑμῖν ὅτι ἐγώ εἰμι εἰ οὖν ἐμὲ ζητεῖτε ἄφετε τούτους ὑπάγειν
verses.king_james: Jesus answered, I have told you that I am *he*: if therefore ye seek me, let these go their way:

verses.latin_vulgate: Ut impleretur sermo, quem dixit: Quia quos dedisti mihi, non perdidi ex eis quemquam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα πληρωθῇ ὁ λόγος ὃν εἶπεν ὅτι οὓς δέδωκάς μοι οὐκ ἀπώλεσα ἐξ αὐτῶν οὐδένα
verses.king_james: That the saying might be fulfilled, which he spake, Of them which thou gavest me have I lost none.

verses.latin_vulgate: Simon ergo Petrus habens gladium eduxit eum: et percussit pontificis servum, et abscidit auriculam ejus dexteram. Erat autem nomen servo Malchus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σίμων οὖν πέτρος ἔχων μάχαιραν εἵλκυσεν αὐτὴν καὶ ἔπαισεν τὸν τοῦ ἀρχιερέως δοῦλον καὶ ἀπέκοψεν αὐτοῦ τὸ ὠτίον τὸ δεξιόν ἦν δὲ ὄνομα τῷ δούλῳ μάλχος
verses.king_james: Then Simon Peter having a sword drew it, and smote the high priest's servant, and cut off his right ear. The servant's name was Malchus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit ergo Jesus Petro: Mitte gladium tuum in vaginam. Calicem, quem dedit mihi Pater, non bibam illum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν οὖν ὁ ἰησοῦς τῷ πέτρῳ βάλε τὴν μάχαιραν σου εἰς τὴν θήκην τὸ ποτήριον ὃ δέδωκέν μοι ὁ πατὴρ οὐ μὴ πίω αὐτό
verses.king_james: Then said Jesus unto Peter, Put up thy sword into the sheath: the cup which my Father hath given me, shall I not drink it?

verses.latin_vulgate: Cohors ergo, et tribunus, et ministri Judæorum comprehenderunt Jesum, et ligaverunt eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ οὖν σπεῖρα καὶ ὁ χιλίαρχος καὶ οἱ ὑπηρέται τῶν ἰουδαίων συνέλαβον τὸν ἰησοῦν καὶ ἔδησαν αὐτὸν
verses.king_james: Then the band and the captain and officers of the Jews took Jesus, and bound him,

verses.latin_vulgate: Et adduxerunt eum ad Annam primum: erat enim socer Caiphæ, qui erat pontifex anni illius.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀπήγαγον αὐτὸν πρὸς ἅνναν πρῶτον ἦν γὰρ πενθερὸς τοῦ καϊάφα ὃς ἦν ἀρχιερεὺς τοῦ ἐνιαυτοῦ ἐκείνου
verses.king_james: And led him away to Annas first; for he was father in law to Caiaphas, which was the high priest that same year.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat autem Caiphas, qui consilium dederat Judæis: Quia expedit unum hominem mori pro populo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δὲ καϊάφας ὁ συμβουλεύσας τοῖς ἰουδαίοις ὅτι συμφέρει ἕνα ἄνθρωπον ἀπολέσθαι ὑπὲρ τοῦ λαοῦ
verses.king_james: Now Caiaphas was he, which gave counsel to the Jews, that it was expedient that one man should die for the people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sequebatur autem Jesum Simon Petrus, et alius discipulus. Discipulus autem ille erat notus pontifici, et introivit cum Jesu in atrium pontificis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἠκολούθει δὲ τῷ ἰησοῦ σίμων πέτρος καὶ ὁ ἄλλος μαθητής ὁ δὲ μαθητὴς ἐκεῖνος ἦν γνωστὸς τῷ ἀρχιερεῖ καὶ συνεισῆλθεν τῷ ἰησοῦ εἰς τὴν αὐλὴν τοῦ ἀρχιερέως
verses.king_james: And Simon Peter followed Jesus, and *so did* another disciple: that disciple was known unto the high priest, and went in with Jesus into the palace of the high priest.

verses.latin_vulgate: Petrus autem stabat ad ostium foris. Exivit ergo discipulus alius, qui erat notus pontifici, et dixit ostiariæ: et introduxit Petrum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ πέτρος εἱστήκει πρὸς τῇ θύρᾳ ἔξω ἐξῆλθεν οὖν ὁ μαθητὴς ὁ ἄλλος ὅς ἦν γνωστὸς τῷ ἀρχιερεῖ, καὶ εἶπεν τῇ θυρωρῷ καὶ εἰσήγαγεν τὸν πέτρον
verses.king_james: But Peter stood at the door without. Then went out that other disciple, which was known unto the high priest, and spake unto her that kept the door, and brought in Peter.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ergo Petro ancilla ostiaria: Numquid et tu ex discipulis es hominis istius? Dicit ille: Non sum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει οὖν ἡ παιδίσκη ἡ θυρωρός τῷ πέτρῳ μὴ καὶ σὺ ἐκ τῶν μαθητῶν εἶ τοῦ ἀνθρώπου τούτου λέγει ἐκεῖνος οὐκ εἰμί
verses.king_james: Then saith the damsel that kept the door unto Peter, Art not thou also *one* of this man's disciples? He saith, I am not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stabant autem servi et ministri ad prunas, quia frigus erat, et calefaciebant se: erat autem cum eis et Petrus stans, et calefaciens se.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἱστήκεισαν δὲ οἱ δοῦλοι καὶ οἱ ὑπηρέται ἀνθρακιὰν πεποιηκότες ὅτι ψῦχος ἦν καὶ ἐθερμαίνοντο ἦν δὲ μετ᾽ αὐτῶν ὁ πέτρος ἑστὼς καὶ θερμαινόμενος
verses.king_james: And the servants and officers stood there, who had made a fire of coals; for it was cold: and they warmed themselves: and Peter stood with them, and warmed himself.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pontifex ergo interrogavit Jesum de discipulis suis, et de doctrina ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ οὖν ἀρχιερεὺς ἠρώτησεν τὸν ἰησοῦν περὶ τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ καὶ περὶ τῆς διδαχῆς αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: The high priest then asked Jesus of his disciples, and of his doctrine.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit ei Jesus: Ego palam locutus sum mundo: ego semper docui in synagoga, et in templo, quo omnes Judæi conveniunt, et in occulto locutus sum nihil.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη αὐτῷ ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐγὼ παῤῥησίᾳ ἐλάλησα τῷ κόσμῳ ἐγὼ πάντοτε ἐδίδαξα ἐν τῇ συναγωγῇ καὶ ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ ὅπου πάντοτε οἱ ἰουδαῖοι συνέρχονται καὶ ἐν κρυπτῷ ελάλησα οὐδέν
verses.king_james: Jesus answered him, I spake openly to the world; I ever taught in the synagogue, and in the temple, whither the Jews always resort; and in secret have I said nothing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid me interrogas? interroga eos qui audierunt quid locutus sim ipsis: ecce hi sciunt quæ dixerim ego.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τί με ἐπερωτᾷς ἐπερώτησον τοὺς ἀκηκοότας τί ἐλάλησα αὐτοῖς ἴδε οὗτοι οἴδασιν ἃ εἶπον ἐγώ
verses.king_james: Why askest thou me? ask them which heard me, what I have said unto them: behold, they know what I said.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc autem cum dixisset, unus assistens ministrorum dedit alapam Jesu, dicens: Sic respondes pontifici?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα δὲ αὐτοῦ εἰπόντος εἷς τῶν ὑπηρετῶν παρεστηκὼς ἔδωκεν ῥάπισμα τῷ ἰησοῦ εἰπών οὕτως ἀποκρίνῃ τῷ ἀρχιερεῖ
verses.king_james: And when he had thus spoken, one of the officers which stood by struck Jesus with the palm of his hand, saying, Answerest thou the high priest so?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit ei Jesus: Si male locutus sum, testimonium perhibe de malo: si autem bene, quid me cædis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη αὐτῷ ὁ ἰησοῦς εἰ κακῶς ἐλάλησα μαρτύρησον περὶ τοῦ κακοῦ εἰ δὲ καλῶς τί με δέρεις
verses.king_james: Jesus answered him, If I have spoken evil, bear witness of the evil: but if well, why smitest thou me?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et misit eum Annas ligatum ad Caipham pontificem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπέστειλεν αὐτὸν ὁ ἅννας δεδεμένον πρὸς καϊάφαν τὸν ἀρχιερέα
verses.king_james: Now Annas had sent him bound unto Caiaphas the high priest.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat autem Simon Petrus stans, et calefaciens se. Dixerunt ergo ei: Numquid et tu ex discipulis ejus es? Negavit ille, et dixit: Non sum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δὲ σίμων πέτρος ἑστὼς καὶ θερμαινόμενος εἶπον οὖν αὐτῷ μὴ καὶ σὺ ἐκ τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ εἶ ἠρνήσατο ἐκεῖνος καὶ εἶπεν οὐκ εἰμί
verses.king_james: And Simon Peter stood and warmed himself. They said therefore unto him, Art not thou also *one* of his disciples? He denied *it*, and said, I am not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ei unus ex servis pontificis, cognatus ejus, cujus abscidit Petrus auriculam: Nonne ego te vidi in horto cum illo?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει εἷς ἐκ τῶν δούλων τοῦ ἀρχιερέως συγγενὴς ὢν οὗ ἀπέκοψεν πέτρος τὸ ὠτίον οὐκ ἐγώ σε εἶδον ἐν τῷ κήπῳ μετ᾽ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: One of the servants of the high priest, being *his* kinsman whose ear Peter cut off, saith, Did not I see thee in the garden with him?

verses.latin_vulgate: Iterum ergo negavit Petrus: et statim gallus cantavit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάλιν οὖν ἠρνήσατο ὁ πέτρος καὶ εὐθέως ἀλέκτωρ ἐφώνησεν
verses.king_james: Peter then denied again: and immediately the cock crew.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adducunt ergo Jesum a Caipha in prætorium. Erat autem mane: et ipsi non introierunt in prætorium, ut non contaminarentur, sed ut manducarent Pascha.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄγουσιν οὖν τὸν ἰησοῦν ἀπὸ τοῦ καϊάφα εἰς τὸ πραιτώριον ἦν δὲ πρωίᾳ καὶ αὐτοὶ οὐκ εἰσῆλθον εἰς τὸ πραιτώριον ἵνα μὴ μιανθῶσιν ἀλλ᾽ ἵνα φάγωσιν τὸ πάσχα
verses.king_james: Then led they Jesus from Caiaphas unto the hall of judgment: and it was early; and they themselves went not into the judgment hall, lest they should be defiled; but that they might eat the passover.

verses.latin_vulgate: Exivit ergo Pilatus ad eos foras, et dixit: Quam accusationem affertis adversus hominem hunc?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐξῆλθεν οὖν ὁ πιλᾶτος πρὸς αὐτοὺς καὶ εἶπεν τίνα κατηγορίαν φέρετε κατὰ τοῦ ἀνθρώπου τούτου
verses.king_james: Pilate then went out unto them, and said, What accusation bring ye against this man?

verses.latin_vulgate: Responderunt, et dixerunt ei: Si non esset hic malefactor, non tibi tradidissemus eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθησαν καὶ εἶπον αὐτῷ εἰ μὴ ἦν οὗτος κακὸποιός, οὐκ ἄν σοι παρεδώκαμεν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: They answered and said unto him, If he were not a malefactor, we would not have delivered him up unto thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit ergo eis Pilatus: Accipite eum vos, et secundum legem vestram judicate eum. Dixerunt ergo ei Judæi: Nobis non licet interficere quemquam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν οὖν αὐτοῖς ὁ πιλᾶτος λάβετε αὐτὸν ὑμεῖς καὶ κατὰ τὸν νόμον ὑμῶν κρίνατε αὐτόν εἶπον οὖν αὐτῷ οἱ ἰουδαῖοι ἡμῖν οὐκ ἔξεστιν ἀποκτεῖναι οὐδένα
verses.king_james: Then said Pilate unto them, Take ye him, and judge him according to your law. The Jews therefore said unto him, It is not lawful for us to put any man to death:

verses.latin_vulgate: Ut sermo Jesu impleretur, quem dixit, significans qua morte esset moriturus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα ὁ λόγος τοῦ ἰησοῦ πληρωθῇ ὃν εἶπεν σημαίνων ποίῳ θανάτῳ ἤμελλεν ἀποθνῄσκειν
verses.king_james: That the saying of Jesus might be fulfilled, which he spake, signifying what death he should die.

verses.latin_vulgate: Introivit ergo iterum in prætorium Pilatus: et vocavit Jesum, et dixit ei: Tu es rex Judæorum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰσῆλθεν οὖν εἰς τὸ πραιτώριον πάλιν ὁ πιλᾶτος καὶ ἐφώνησεν τὸν ἰησοῦν καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ σὺ εἶ ὁ βασιλεὺς τῶν ἰουδαίων
verses.king_james: Then Pilate entered into the judgment hall again, and called Jesus, and said unto him, Art thou the King of the Jews?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Jesus: A temetipso hoc dicis, an alii dixerunt tibi de me?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη αὐτῷ ὁ ἰησοῦς ἀφ᾽ ἑαυτοῦ σὺ τοῦτο λέγεις ἢ ἄλλοι σοι εἶπόν περὶ ἐμοῦ
verses.king_james: Jesus answered him, Sayest thou this thing of thyself, or did others tell it thee of me?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Pilatus: Numquid ego Judæus sum? gens tua et pontifices tradiderunt te mihi: quid fecisti?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ὁ πιλᾶτος μήτι ἐγὼ ἰουδαῖός εἰμι τὸ ἔθνος τὸ σὸν καὶ οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς παρέδωκάν σε ἐμοί τί ἐποίησας
verses.king_james: Pilate answered, Am I a Jew? Thine own nation and the chief priests have delivered thee unto me: what hast thou done?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Jesus: Regnum meum non est de hoc mundo. Si ex hoc mundo esset regnum meum, ministri mei utique decertarent ut non traderer Judæis: nunc autem regnum meum non est hinc.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ὁ ἰησοῦς ἡ βασιλεία ἡ ἐμὴ οὐκ ἔστιν ἐκ τοῦ κόσμου τούτου εἰ ἐκ τοῦ κόσμου τούτου ἦν ἡ βασιλεία ἡ ἐμή οἱ ὑπηρέται ἄν οἱ ἐμοὶ ἠγωνίζοντο ἵνα μὴ παραδοθῶ τοῖς ἰουδαίοις νῦν δὲ ἡ βασιλεία ἡ ἐμὴ οὐκ ἔστιν ἐντεῦθεν
verses.king_james: Jesus answered, My kingdom is not of this world: if my kingdom were of this world, then would my servants fight, that I should not be delivered to the Jews: but now is my kingdom not from hence.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit itaque ei Pilatus: Ergo rex es tu? Respondit Jesus: Tu dicis quia rex sum ego. Ego in hoc natus sum, et ad hoc veni in mundum, ut testimonium perhibeam veritati: omnis qui est ex veritate, audit vocem meam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν οὖν αὐτῷ ὁ πιλᾶτος οὐκοῦν βασιλεὺς εἶ σύ ἀπεκρίθη ὁ ἰησοῦς σὺ λέγεις ὅτι βασιλεύς εἰμι ἐγὼ ἐγὼ εἰς τοῦτο γεγέννημαι καὶ εἰς τοῦτο ἐλήλυθα εἰς τὸν κόσμον ἵνα μαρτυρήσω τῇ ἀληθείᾳ πᾶς ὁ ὢν ἐκ τῆς ἀληθείας ἀκούει μου τῆς φωνῆς
verses.king_james: Pilate therefore said unto him, Art thou a king then? Jesus answered, Thou sayest that I am a king. To this end was I born, and for this cause came I into the world, that I should bear witness unto the truth. Every one that is of the truth heareth my voice.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ei Pilatus: Quid est veritas? Et cum hoc dixisset, iterum exivit ad Judæos, et dicit eis: Ego nullam invenio in eo causam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῷ ὁ πιλᾶτος τί ἐστιν ἀλήθεια καὶ τοῦτο εἰπὼν πάλιν ἐξῆλθεν πρὸς τοὺς ἰουδαίους καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς ἐγὼ οὐδεμίαν αἰτίαν εὑρίσκω ἐν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: Pilate saith unto him, What is truth? And when he had said this, he went out again unto the Jews, and saith unto them, I find in him no fault *at all*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Est autem consuetudo vobis ut unum dimittam vobis in Pascha: vultis ergo dimittam vobis regem Judæorum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔστιν δὲ συνήθεια ὑμῖν ἵνα ἕνα ὑμῖν ἀπολύσω ἐν τῷ πάσχα βούλεσθε οὖν ὑμῖν ἀπολύσω τὸν βασιλέα τῶν ἰουδαίων
verses.king_james: But ye have a custom, that I should release unto you one at the passover: will ye therefore that I release unto you the King of the Jews?

verses.latin_vulgate: Clamaverunt ergo rursum omnes, dicentes: Non hunc, sed Barabbam. Erat autem Barabbas latro.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκραύγασαν οὖν πάλιν πάντες, λέγοντες μὴ τοῦτον ἀλλὰ τὸν βαραββᾶν ἦν δὲ ὁ βαραββᾶς λῃστής
verses.king_james: Then cried they all again, saying, Not this man, but Barabbas. Now Barabbas was a robber.
